sourceMovie.title: Anthropoid
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Anthropoid (2016) stuck because it commits to its history premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Sean Ellis's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The history and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Man with the Iron Heart
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Anthropoid clicked for you, The Man with the Iron Heart (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Valkyrie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Anthropoid clicked for you, Valkyrie (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Anthropoid?
faq.answer: Try The Man with the Iron Heart, Valkyrie, Defiance—each shares DNA with Anthropoid while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Anthropoid?
faq.answer: Anthropoid (2016) is best described as History, Thriller, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Anthropoid worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
